title: Native Language Specialists – Japanese, Korean, and French (3-Month GIG Contract)
summary: This challenge is intended to source highly qualified native speakers for a 3-month contract position working full-time (8 hours/day) to support operations. The selected candidates will be part of an urgent initiative and are expected to support global projects. This opportunity is location agnostic

artifacts.design_doc_md:
## Problem Summary
This challenge is intended to source highly qualified native speakers for a 3-month contract position working full-time (8 hours/day) to support operations. The selected candidates will be part of an urgent initiative and are expected to support global projects. This opportunity is location agnostic.

## Business Context:
This gig is part of an urgent operational effort. The candidate(s) will support key client operations with native language proficiency and high communication skills, ensuring operational excellence in Japanese, Korean, or French markets.

## Scope of Work
* Duration:  3 month

## Proposed Approach

## API / Interface Changes
- Derived from statement: This challenge is intended to source highly qualified native speakers for a 3-month contract position working full-time (8 hours/day) to support operations. The selected candidates will be part of an 

## Constraints & SLAs
- Latency < 500ms
- Availability 99.5%
- Budget: reuse existing services

## Risks & Trade-offs
- Limited observability when reusing legacy APIs
- Trade-off between cost and performance

## Edge Cases
- Stress test under bursty load
- Handle malformed payloads gracefully
- Why it failed: No repo plan content detected | Missing: Problem Summary, Plan, Risks, Validation.
Next steps: address each missing rubric/finding, add explicit risks/test plans, and tighten acceptance criteria.
- Why it failed: Plan present; Patch/application guidance provided | Missing: Problem Summary, Plan, Risks, Validation.
Next steps: address each missing rubric/finding, add explicit risks/test plans, and tighten acceptance criteria.
- Why it failed: Schema defined; Pipeline steps listed; Data quality or invariants addressed; Validation queries present; Sample outputs described | Missing: Sample Outputs, Acceptance Checklist.
Next steps: address each missing rubric/finding, add explicit risks/test plans, and tighten acceptance criteria.

## Acceptance Checklist
- Architecture diagrams reviewed
- APIs documented
- Smoke tests executed
interfaces: Recommendation Service – stateless API using feature store
tradeoffs: Server-side rendering vs SPA for personalization UX
Managed message bus vs self-hosted Kafka

plan: Summarize router rationale and challenge context.
Describe architecture sections with risks + constraints.
Provide acceptance checklist with validation guidance.
